prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of the computer—it’s the little part inside that does all the thinking. When you click on something or type a message, the CPU figures out what to do next, just like how I decide whether to bake cookies or go for a walk. It doesn’t store your photos or songs—that’s what the memory and hard drive are for—but it makes everything happen by following instructions super fast. Even though I don’t quite get all the fancy tech talk, I know the CPU is mighty important, like the conductor of an orchestra, making sure all the parts play together nicely!
